title: Lowest Common Ancestor in Binary Tree

content.problemStatement:
Given a binary tree and two node values n1n1n1 and n2n2n2, find their lowest common ancestor (LCA). The LCA is defined as the deepest node in the tree that is an ancestor of both nodes.


Input Format

First line contains TTT, the number of test cases
For each test case:

First line contains NNN, the number of nodes in the binary tree
Second line contains NNN space-separated integers representing the level-order traversal of the tree (null nodes represented by -1)
Third line contains two integers n1n1n1 and n2n2n2




Output Format

For each test case, print the value of the lowest common ancestor node.
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains N, the number of nodes in the binary tree. The second line contains N space-separated integers representing the level-order traversal of the tree (null nodes represented by -1). The third line contains two integers n1 and n2.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the value of the lowest common ancestor node.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1:

LCA of 5 and 6 is 2

Test Case 2:

LCA of 4 and 5 is 2

Test Case 3:

LCA of 2 and 3 is 1

solution.approach: To find the lowest common ancestor of two nodes in a binary tree, we can use a recursive approach. We start by checking if the current node is null. If it is, we return null. If the current node's value is equal to either n1 or n2, we return the current node. Then, we recursively call the function on the left and right subtrees. If both recursive calls return non-null values, it means that n1 and n2 are in different subtrees, so we return the current node as the lowest common ancestor. If only one recursive call returns a non-null value, we return that value as the lowest common ancestor.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, where N is the number of nodes in the binary tree. This is because in the worst case, we need to traverse the entire tree.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) due to the recursive call stack.
